mainEntity.name: What if I quit my job after getting hurt by a falling object? Does my claim die?

acceptedAnswer.text: Your medical benefits continue even if you quit, but you may lose your right to lost wage payments. Consult a lawyer before resigning to ensure you don't inadvertently forfeit thousands of dollars in compensation.
acceptedAnswer.description:
If you quit your job after being hit by an object, you are still entitled to medical benefits for that injury. Your medical care is tied to the date of the accident, not your ongoing employment status. However, quitting can make it very difficult to collect "lost wage" (disability) benefits. If you quit, the insurance company will argue that you aren't working because you chose to leave, not because of your injury.

It is almost always better to stay employed while your claim is pending, or at least wait until a doctor places you on "total disability" before leaving. Quoting due to "frustration" or "pain" without a doctor's order can be a costly mistake.

Key factors include the reason for leaving and your current medical restrictions. If the environment is truly unsafe, you should consult an attorney before resigning to protect your future compensation. Your medical rights remain, but your wage rights are at risk. A lawyer can guide you on the best timing for a career change that doesn't forfeit your settlement. Protecting your financial future requires a strategic approach to your employment status.
